publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Local hooks and server checks; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Local hooks and server checks: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A pre-commit hook guarantees every commit in the remote passed the check and is safe to trust.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat hooks as convenience and defense in depth, distribute them transparently, minimize privilege, and enforce non-bypassable policy on trusted CI or server controls.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Treat hooks as convenience and defense in depth, distribute them transparently, minimize privilege, and enforce non-bypassable policy on trusted CI or server controls.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Local hooks and server checks instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.
